howItWorks.intro: This marathon pace calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: What is a good marathon time?
faq.answer: Average finish is about 4:30. Sub-4:00 is solid, sub-3:30 is competitive, sub-3:00 is elite amateur, and sub-2:30 is professional territory.

faq.question: Should I run even splits?
faq.answer: Yes — running the same pace throughout is the most efficient strategy. Negative splits (faster second half) are even better but harder to execute.

faq.question: How do I train for my goal pace?
faq.answer: Long runs at 30-60 seconds slower than goal pace. Tempo runs at goal pace. Speed work at faster than goal pace. Build up to 18-20 mile long runs.

faq.question: What about the wall at mile 20?
faq.answer: The wall happens when glycogen stores deplete. Prevent it with proper nutrition (carb loading, race-day fueling with gels every 45 min) and adequate training.

faq.question: Is the Marathon Pace Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Marathon Pace Calculator?
sections.body: The Marathon Pace Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes and fitness enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the fitness and training space. By entering your goal time, goal time, you get instant results including required pace, 5k split, half marathon split. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting required pace right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In fitness and training, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes and fitness enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your training with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your goal time and need to find the right required pace. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.description: Type or select your goal time in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

tips: Double-check your goal time before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
